piy'āpāyo hi pāpako,
ganthā tesaṃ na vijjanti,
yesaṃ n'atthi piy'āppiyaṃ.

Return JSON for: tasmā piyaṃ na kayirātha,
piy'āpāyo hi pāpako,
ganthā tesaṃ na vijjanti,
yesaṃ n'atthi piy'āppiyaṃ.

disambiguation.rationale: In this Dhammapada verse (DHP 211), 'tasmā' is used as an indeclinable conjunction/adverb meaning 'therefore' or 'that is why', linking the advice to the preceding context.
Here 'piya' functions as an adjective in the masculine accusative singular, qualifying the implied object of the verb 'kayirātha' (should hold dear/pleasant).
This is the standard negative particle 'na' meaning 'not'.

This is the optative 3rd person singular form (reflx opt 3rd sg) of the verb √kar (under lemma 'kayirā' or 'karoti'), meaning 'one should do' or 'one should make' ('na kayirātha': one should not make/hold [dear]).
A genitive determinative compound (tappurisa) 'piyassa apāyo' meaning 'separation from the beloved' or 'loss of loved ones', in the nominative singular.
Here 'hi' is used in the explanatory sense meaning 'for' or 'because', explaining why one should not hold things dear.
An adjective meaning 'bad', 'evil', or 'harmful' in the masculine nominative singular, agreeing with 'piyāpāyo'.
Refers to 'mental bonds', 'knots', or 'ties' (gantha 1) in the masculine nominative plural, which do not exist for those who have no likes or dislikes.
Genitive plural masculine of the demonstrative pronoun 'ta' ('for them', 'their').
